debug_info.goal:
α : Type u_1
β : Type u_2
γ : Type u_3
inst✝ : TopologicalSpace α
x : α
p p' q q' : α → Prop
hT : {x | p x} ∈ nhds x
hT' : {x | p' x} ∈ nhds x
hS : {x | q x} ∈ nhds x
hS' : {x | q' x} ∈ nhds x
⊢ {x | p x ∧ q x ∨ p' x ∧ q' x} ∈ nhds x

proof:
have h1 : ∃ s ∈ 𝓝 x, ∀ y ∈ s, p y := by
    refine' ⟨{x | p x}, hT, _⟩
    intro y hy
    exact hy
  have h2 : ∃ s ∈ 𝓝 x, ∀ y ∈ s, p' y := by
    refine' ⟨{x | p' x}, hT', _⟩
    intro y hy
    exact hy
  have h3 : ∃ s ∈ 𝓝 x, ∀ y ∈ s, q y := by
    refine' ⟨{x | q x}, hS, _⟩
    intro y hy
    exact hy
  have h4 : ∃ s ∈ 𝓝 x, ∀ y ∈ s, q' y := by
    refine' ⟨{x | q' x}, hS', _⟩
    intro y hy
    exact hy
  -- Use the fact that the intersection of finitely many neighborhoods is a neighborhood
  -- to find a common neighborhood where all conditions are satisfied.
  filter_upwards [hT, hT', hS, hS'] with y hy1 hy2 hy3 hy4
  -- For each y in the common neighborhood, at least one of the two conditions must hold.
  have h5 : p y ∧ q y ∨ p' y ∧ q' y := by
    by_cases h : p y
    · -- Case: p y holds
      by_cases h' : q y
      · -- Subcase: q y holds
        exact Or.inl ⟨h, h'⟩
      · -- Subcase: q y does not hold
        exfalso
        -- If q y does not hold, then y is not in {x | q x}, contradicting hy3.
        have h6 : q y := by
          have h7 : y ∈ {x | q x} := hy3
          exact h7
        contradiction
    · -- Case: p y does not hold
      by_cases h' : p' y
      · -- Subcase: p' y holds
        by_cases h'' : q' y
        · -- Subsubcase: q' y holds
          exact Or.inr ⟨h', h''⟩
        · -- Subsubcase: q' y does not hold
          exfalso
          -- If q' y does not hold, then y is not in {x | q' x}, contradicting hy4.
          have h6 : q' y := by
            have h7 : y ∈ {x | q' x} := hy4
            exact h7
          contradiction
      · -- Subcase: p' y does not hold
        exfalso
        -- If p' y does not hold, then y is not in {x | p' x}, contradicting hy2.
        have h6 : p' y := by
          have h7 : y ∈ {x | p' x} := hy2
          exact h7
        contradiction
  -- The result follows directly from the cases considered.
  exact h5
failed_attempts:
have h1 : {x | p x ∧ q x} ∈ 𝓝 x := by
    have h1 : {x | p x ∧ q x} = {x | p x} ∩ {x | q x} := by
      ext
      simp [and_comm]
      <;> tauto
    rw [h1]
    exact hT.inf hS
  have h2 : {x | p' x ∧ q' x} ∈ 𝓝 x := by
    have h2 : {x | p' x ∧ q' x} = {x | p' x} ∩ {x | q' x} := by
      ext
      simp [and_comm]
      <;> tauto
    rw [h2]
    exact hT'.inf hS'
  have h3 : {x | p x ∧ q x ∨ p' x ∧ q' x} ∈ 𝓝 x := by
    have h3 : {x | p x ∧ q x ∨ p' x ∧ q' x} ⊇ ({x | p x ∧ q x} : Set α) ∪ ({x | p' x ∧ q' x} : Set α) := by
      intro x hx
      simp only [Set.mem_union, Set.mem_setOf_eq] at hx ⊢
      tauto
    exact h1.sup h2 |>.mono h3
  exact h3

have h₁ : {x | p x} ∈ 𝓝 x := hT
  have h₂ : {x | p' x} ∈ 𝓝 x := hT'
  have h₃ : {x | q x} ∈ 𝓝 x := hS
  have h₄ : {x | q' x} ∈ 𝓝 x := hS'
  -- We will use the fact that the intersection of sets in the neighborhood filter is in the neighborhood filter.
  have h₅ : ({x | p x} ∩ {x | q x}) ∈ 𝓝 x := by
    -- The intersection of {x | p x} and {x | q x} is in the neighborhood filter.
    apply Filter.inter_mem h₁ h₃
  have h₆ : ({x | p' x} ∩ {x | q' x}) ∈ 𝓝 x := by
    -- The intersection of {x | p' x} and {x | q' x} is in the neighborhood filter.
    apply Filter.inter_mem h₂ h₄
  -- Now, we need to show that the union of the two intersections is in the neighborhood filter.
  have h₇ : ({x | p x} ∩ {x | q x}) ∪ ({x | p' x} ∩ {x | q' x}) ∈ 𝓝 x := by
    -- Since both sets are in the neighborhood filter, their union is also in the neighborhood filter.
    apply Filter.union_mem h₅ h₆
  -- We need to show that the set {x | p x ∧ q x ∨ p' x ∧ q' x} is equal to the union of the two intersections.
  have h₈ : {x : α | p x ∧ q x ∨ p' x ∧ q' x} = ({x : α | p x} ∩ {x : α | q x}) ∪ ({x : α | p' x} ∩ {x : α | q' x}) := by
    -- Prove that the two sets are equal by showing that each is a subset of the other.
    ext z
    simp only [Set.mem_union, Set.mem_setOf_eq, Set.mem_inter_iff]
    <;>
    tauto
  -- Now we can use the equality to show that the set {x | p x ∧ q x ∨ p' x ∧ q' x} is in the neighborhood filter.
  rw [h₈]
  exact h₇

have h1 : {x | p x ∧ q x} ∈ 𝓝 x := by
    have h1₁ : {x | p x ∧ q x} = {x | p x} ∩ {x | q x} := by
      ext x
      simp [and_comm]
      <;> tauto
    rw [h1₁]
    -- Use the fact that the intersection of two neighborhoods is a neighborhood
    have h1₂ : {x | p x} ∈ 𝓝 x := hT
    have h1₃ : {x | q x} ∈ 𝓝 x := hS
    -- The intersection of two neighborhoods is a neighborhood
    exact inf_mem h1₂ h1₃
  
  have h2 : {x | p' x ∧ q' x} ∈ 𝓝 x := by
    have h2₁ : {x | p' x ∧ q' x} = {x | p' x} ∩ {x | q' x} := by
      ext x
      simp [and_comm]
      <;> tauto
    rw [h2₁]
    -- Use the fact that the intersection of two neighborhoods is a neighborhood
    have h2₂ : {x | p' x} ∈ 𝓝 x := hT'
    have h2₃ : {x | q' x} ∈ 𝓝 x := hS'
    -- The intersection of two neighborhoods is a neighborhood
    exact inf_mem h2₂ h2₃
  
  -- Use the fact that the union of two neighborhoods is a neighborhood
  have h3 : {x | p x ∧ q x ∨ p' x ∧ q' x} = {x | p x ∧ q x} ∪ {x | p' x ∧ q' x} := by
    ext x
    simp [or_comm]
    <;> tauto
  
  rw [h3]
  -- The union of two neighborhoods is a neighborhood
  have h4 : {x | p x ∧ q x} ∈ 𝓝 x := h1
  have h5 : {x | p' x ∧ q' x} ∈ 𝓝 x := h2
  -- Use the fact that the union of two neighborhoods is a neighborhood
  exact sup_mem h4 h5

have h₁ : {x : α | p x ∧ q x} ∈ 𝓝 x := by
    have h₁₁ : {x : α | p x} ∈ 𝓝 x := hT
    have h₁₂ : {x : α | q x} ∈ 𝓝 x := hS
    have h₁₃ : {x : α | p x ∧ q x} ∈ 𝓝 x := by
      -- The intersection of two neighborhoods is a neighborhood
      have h₁₄ : {x : α | p x ∧ q x} = {x : α | p x} ∩ {x : α | q x} := by
        ext x
        simp [and_imp]
        <;> aesop
      rw [h₁₄]
      exact h₁₁.inter h₁₂
    exact h₁₃
  have h₂ : {x : α | p' x ∧ q' x} ∈ 𝓝 x := by
    have h₂₁ : {x : α | p' x} ∈ 𝓝 x := hT'
    have h₂₂ : {x : α | q' x} ∈ 𝓝 x := hS'
    have h₂₃ : {x : α | p' x ∧ q' x} ∈ 𝓝 x := by
      -- The intersection of two neighborhoods is a neighborhood
      have h₂₄ : {x : α | p' x ∧ q' x} = {x : α | p' x} ∩ {x : α | q' x} := by
        ext x
        simp [and_imp]
        <;> aesop
      rw [h₂₄]
      exact h₂₁.inter h₂₂
    exact h₂₃
  -- The union of two neighborhoods is a neighborhood
  have h₃ : {x : α | p x ∧ q x ∨ p' x ∧ q' x} ∈ 𝓝 x := by
    have h₃₁ : {x : α | p x ∧ q x ∨ p' x ∧ q' x} = {x : α | p x ∧ q x} ∪ {x : α | p' x ∧ q' x} := by
      ext x
      simp [or_imp]
      <;> aesop
    rw [h₃₁]
    exact h₁.union h₂
  exact h₃

have h1 : ({x | p x ∧ q x} : Set α) ∈ 𝓝 x := by
    have h1 : ({x | p x} : Set α) ∈ 𝓝 x := hT
    have h2 : ({x | q x} : Set α) ∈ 𝓝 x := hS
    exact h1.inf h2
  have h2 : ({x | p' x ∧ q' x} : Set α) ∈ 𝓝 x := by
    have h1 : ({x | p' x} : Set α) ∈ 𝓝 x := hT'
    have h2 : ({x | q' x} : Set α) ∈ 𝓝 x := hS'
    exact h1.inf h2
  have h3 : ({x | p x ∧ q x ∨ p' x ∧ q' x} : Set α) ∈ 𝓝 x := by
    -- We use the fact that the union of two neighborhoods is a neighborhood
    have h4 : ({x | p x ∧ q x} : Set α) ∪ {x | p' x ∧ q' x} ∈ 𝓝 x := by
      -- The union of two neighborhoods is a neighborhood
      exact h1.sup h2
    -- The set {x | p x ∧ q x ∨ p' x ∧ q' x} is equal to the union of the two sets
    have h5 : ({x | p x ∧ q x ∨ p' x ∧ q' x} : Set α) = ({x | p x ∧ q x} : Set α) ∪ {x | p' x ∧ q' x} := by
      ext x
      simp [or_and_right]
      <;> tauto
    -- Therefore, it is a neighborhood of x
    rw [h5]
    exact h4
  exact h3

have h₁ : {x | p x ∧ q x} ∈ 𝓝 x := by
    -- Since both {x | p x} and {x | q x} are in 𝓝 x, their intersection is also in 𝓝 x.
    have h₁ : {x | p x} ∈ 𝓝 x := hT
    have h₂ : {x | q x} ∈ 𝓝 x := hS
    have h₃ : {x | p x ∧ q x} ∈ 𝓝 x := by
      -- Use the fact that the intersection of two neighborhoods is a neighborhood.
      have h₄ : {x | p x} ∩ {x | q x} ∈ 𝓝 x := by
        apply Filter.inter_mem
        exact h₁
        exact h₂
      -- The intersection can also be written as {x | p x ∧ q x}.
      have h₅ : {x | p x} ∩ {x | q x} = {x | p x ∧ q x} := by
        ext x
        simp [and_comm]
        <;>
        tauto
      rw [h₅] at h₄
      exact h₄
    exact h₃
  
  have h₂ : {x | p' x ∧ q' x} ∈ 𝓝 x := by
    have h₁ : {x | p' x} ∈ 𝓝 x := hT'
    have h₂ : {x | q' x} ∈ 𝓝 x := hS'
    have h₃ : {x | p' x ∧ q' x} ∈ 𝓝 x := by
      have h₄ : {x | p' x} ∩ {x | q' x} ∈ 𝓝 x := by
        apply Filter.inter_mem
        exact h₁
        exact h₂
      have h₅ : {x | p' x} ∩ {x | q' x} = {x | p' x ∧ q' x} := by
        ext x
        simp [and_comm]
        <;>
        tauto
      rw [h₅] at h₄
      exact h₄
    exact h₃
  
  -- Use the fact that the union of two neighborhoods is a neighborhood.
  have h₃ : {x | p x ∧ q x ∨ p' x ∧ q' x} ∈ 𝓝 x := by
    have h₄ : {x | p x ∧ q x} ∪ {x | p' x ∧ q' x} ∈ 𝓝 x := by
      apply Filter.union_mem
      exact h₁
      exact h₂
    -- The union can also be written as {x | p x ∧ q x ∨ p' x ∧ q' x}.
    have h₅ : {x | p x ∧ q x} ∪ {x | p' x ∧ q' x} = {x | p x ∧ q x ∨ p' x ∧ q' x} := by
      ext x
      simp [or_comm]
      <;>
      tauto
    rw [h₅] at h₄
    exact h₄
  
  exact h₃

have h₁ : {x : α | p x} ∈ 𝓝 x := hT
  have h₂ : {x : α | p' x} ∈ 𝓝 x := hT'
  have h₃ : {x : α | q x} ∈ 𝓝 x := hS
  have h₄ : {x : α | q' x} ∈ 𝓝 x := hS'
  have h₅ : ({x : α | p x} ∩ {x : α | q x}) ∈ 𝓝 x := by
    apply mem_nhds_inter h₁ h₃
  have h₆ : ({x : α | p' x} ∩ {x : α | q' x}) ∈ 𝓝 x := by
    apply mem_nhds_inter h₂ h₄
  have h₇ : ({x : α | p x ∧ q x} ∪ {x : α | p' x ∧ q' x}) ∈ 𝓝 x := by
    have h₈ : {x : α | p x ∧ q x} = {x : α | p x} ∩ {x : α | q x} := by
      ext x
      simp [and_comm]
      <;> aesop
    have h₉ : {x : α | p' x ∧ q' x} = {x : α | p' x} ∩ {x : α | q' x} := by
      ext x
      simp [and_comm]
      <;> aesop
    rw [h₈, h₉]
    have h₁₀ : ({x : α | p x} ∩ {x : α | q x}) ∈ 𝓝 x := h₅
    have h₁₁ : ({x : α | p' x} ∩ {x : α | q' x}) ∈ 𝓝 x := h₆
    have h₁₂ : ({x : α | p x} ∩ {x : α | q x}) ∪ ({x : α | p' x} ∩ {x : α | q' x}) ∈ 𝓝 x := by
      apply mem_nhds_union h₁₀ h₁₁
    exact h₁₂
  have h₈ : ({x : α | p x ∧ q x ∨ p' x ∧ q' x}) = ({x : α | p x ∧ q x} ∪ {x : α | p' x ∧ q' x}) := by
    ext x
    simp [or_comm]
    <;> aesop
  rw [h₈]
  exact h₇

have h1 : {x | p x ∧ q x} ∈ 𝓝 x := by
    have h2 : {x | p x} ∈ 𝓝 x := hT
    have h3 : {x | q x} ∈ 𝓝 x := hS
    have h4 : {x | p x ∧ q x} ∈ 𝓝 x := by
      apply Filter.inter_mem h2 h3
    exact h4
  have h5 : {x | p' x ∧ q' x} ∈ 𝓝 x := by
    have h6 : {x | p' x} ∈ 𝓝 x := hT'
    have h7 : {x | q' x} ∈ 𝓝 x := hS'
    have h8 : {x | p' x ∧ q' x} ∈ 𝓝 x := by
      apply Filter.inter_mem h6 h7
    exact h8
  have h9 : {x | p x ∧ q x ∨ p' x ∧ q' x} ∈ 𝓝 x := by
    have h10 : {x | p x ∧ q x} ∈ 𝓝 x := h1
    have h11 : {x | p' x ∧ q' x} ∈ 𝓝 x := h5
    have h12 : {x | p x ∧ q x ∨ p' x ∧ q' x} ∈ 𝓝 x := by
      apply Filter.mem_union h10 h11
    exact h12
  exact h9

have h₁ : {x | p x ∧ q x} ∈ 𝓝 x := by
    -- Prove that the intersection of {x | p x} and {x | q x} is in the neighborhood filter of x
    have h₂ : {x | p x ∧ q x} = {x | p x} ∩ {x | q x} := by
      ext x
      simp [and_comm]
    rw [h₂]
    -- Use the fact that the intersection of two sets in the neighborhood filter is in the neighborhood filter
    exact 𝓝.inter_mem hT hS
  have h₂ : {x | p' x ∧ q' x} ∈ 𝓝 x := by
    -- Prove that the intersection of {x | p' x} and {x | q' x} is in the neighborhood filter of x
    have h₃ : {x | p' x ∧ q' x} = {x | p' x} ∩ {x | q' x} := by
      ext x
      simp [and_comm]
    rw [h₃]
    -- Use the fact that the intersection of two sets in the neighborhood filter is in the neighborhood filter
    exact 𝓝.inter_mem hT' hS'
  -- Prove that the union of {x | p x ∧ q x} and {x | p' x ∧ q' x} is in the neighborhood filter of x
  have h₃ : {x | p x ∧ q x ∨ p' x ∧ q' x} = {x | p x ∧ q x} ∪ {x | p' x ∧ q' x} := by
    ext x
    simp [or_comm]
    <;> tauto
  rw [h₃]
  -- Use the fact that the union of two sets in the neighborhood filter is in the neighborhood filter
  exact 𝓝.union_mem h₁ h₂

have h1 : {x : α | p x} ∩ {x : α | q x} ∈ nhds x := by
    apply Filter.inter_mem
    exact hT
    exact hS
  -- Now we need to show that {x : α | p x} ∩ {x : α | q x} = {x : α | p x ∧ q x}
  -- But this is true, because membership is equivalent.
  rw [Set.ext_iff] at *
  simp_all [Set.mem_inter_iff]
  <;> aesop

have h₃₁ : ({x : α | p x ∧ q x} : Set α) ∈ 𝓝 x := by sorry
  have h₃₂ : ({x : α | p' x ∧ q' x} : Set α) ∈ 𝓝 x := by sorry
  have h₃₃ : {x | p x ∧ q x ∨ p' x ∧ q' x} = {x : α | p x ∧ q x} ∪ {x : α | p' x ∧ q' x} := by sorry
  rw [h₃₃]
  exact Filter.union_mem h₃₁ h₃₂

have h₁ : {x | p x ∧ q x} ∈ 𝓝 x := by
    have h₂ : {x | p x} ∈ 𝓝 x := hT
    have h₃ : {x | q x} ∈ 𝓝 x := hS
    have h₄ : {x | p x ∧ q x} = {x | p x} ∩ {x | q x} := by
      ext x
      simp [and_comm]
      <;> aesop
    rw [h₄]
    exact Filter.inter_mem h₂ h₃
  have h₂ : {x | p' x ∧ q' x} ∈ 𝓝 x := by
    have h₃ : {x | p' x} ∈ 𝓝 x := hT'
    have h₄ : {x | q' x} ∈ 𝓝 x := hS'
    have h₅ : {x | p' x ∧ q' x} = {x | p' x} ∩ {x | q' x} := by
      ext x
      simp [and_comm]
      <;> aesop
    rw [h₅]
    exact Filter.inter_mem h₃ h₄
  have h₃ : {x | p x ∧ q x ∨ p' x ∧ q' x} = {x | p x ∧ q x} ∪ {x | p' x ∧ q' x} := by
    ext x
    <;> simp [or_comm]
    <;> tauto
  rw [h₃]
  exact Filter.union_mem h₁ h₂

have h₁ : {x | p x ∧ q x} ∈ 𝓝 x := by
    have h₂ : {x | p x} ∈ 𝓝 x := hT
    have h₃ : {x | q x} ∈ 𝓝 x := hS
    have h₄ : {x | p x ∧ q x} = {x | p x} ∩ {x | q x} := by
      ext x
      simp [and_imp]
      <;> tauto
    rw [h₄]
    exact nhds.inter_mem h₂ h₃
  have h₂ : {x | p' x ∧ q' x} ∈ 𝓝 x := by
    have h₃ : {x | p' x} ∈ 𝓝 x := hT'
    have h₄ : {x | q' x} ∈ 𝓝 x := hS'
    have h₅ : {x | p' x ∧ q' x} = {x | p' x} ∩ {x | q' x} := by
      ext x
      simp [and_imp]
      <;> tauto
    rw [h₅]
    exact nhds.inter_mem h₃ h₄
  have h₃ : {x | p x ∧ q x ∨ p' x ∧ q' x} = {x | p x ∧ q x} ∪ {x | p' x ∧ q' x} := by
    ext x
    <;> simp [or_and_right]
    <;> tauto
  rw [h₃]
  exact nhds.union_mem h₁ h₂

-- Use the fact that the intersection of two neighborhoods is a neighborhood to find a smaller neighborhood contained in both {x | p x} and {x | q x}.
  have h₁ : {x | p x} ∩ {x | q x} ∈ 𝓝 x := by
    -- Since {x | p x} and {x | q x} are neighborhoods of x, their intersection is also a neighborhood of x.
    have h₁ : ({x | p x} ∈ 𝓝 x) := hT
    have h₂ : ({x | q x} ∈ 𝓝 x) := hS
    -- Use the property of filters that intersections of neighborhoods are neighborhoods.
    exact h₁.inter h₂
  -- Similarly, the intersection of {x | p' x} and {x | q' x} is a neighborhood of x.
  have h₂ : {x | p' x} ∩ {x | q' x} ∈ 𝓝 x := by
    have h₁ : ({x | p' x} ∈ 𝓝 x) := hT'
    have h₂ : ({x | q' x} ∈ 𝓝 x) := hS'
    exact h₁.inter h₂
  -- The union of these two intersections is a neighborhood of x and is contained in {x | p x ∧ q x ∨ p' x ∧ q' x}.
  have h₃ : ({x | p x ∧ q x} ∪ {x | p' x ∧ q' x}) ∈ 𝓝 x := by
    -- Use the fact that the union of two neighborhoods is a neighborhood if at least one of them is a neighborhood.
    have h₃ : {x | p x ∧ q x} = {x | p x} ∩ {x | q x} := by
      ext x
      simp [and_comm]
      <;> aesop
    have h₄ : {x | p' x ∧ q' x} = {x | p' x} ∩ {x | q' x} := by
      ext x
      simp [and_comm]
      <;> aesop
    rw [h₃, h₄]
    -- The union of two neighborhoods is a neighborhood if at least one of them is a neighborhood.
    exact h₁.union h₂
  -- Since {x | p x ∧ q x ∨ p' x ∧ q' x} contains the union of the two intersections, it is also a neighborhood of x.
  have h₄ : ({x | p x ∧ q x ∨ p' x ∧ q' x} : Set α) ∈ 𝓝 x := by
    have h₅ : ({x | p x ∧ q x} ∪ {x | p' x ∧ q' x}) ⊆ {x | p x ∧ q x ∨ p' x ∧ q' x} := by
      intro x hx
      simp only [Set.mem_union, Set.mem_setOf_eq] at hx ⊢
      tauto
    -- Use the fact that if a neighborhood is contained in a set, then that set is also a neighborhood.
    have h₆ : ({x | p x ∧ q x} ∪ {x | p' x ∧ q' x}) ∈ 𝓝 x := h₃
    exact h₆.mono h₅
  -- The result follows.
  simpa using h₄

have h₁ : {x | p x ∧ q x} ∈ 𝓝 x := by
    have h₂ : {x | p x} ∈ 𝓝 x := hT
    have h₃ : {x | q x} ∈ 𝓝 x := hS
    have h₄ : {x | p x ∧ q x} ∈ 𝓝 x := by
      have h₅ : {x | p x ∧ q x} = {x | p x} ∩ {x | q x} := by
        ext x
        simp
      rw [h₅]
      exact nhds.inter_mem h₂ h₃
    exact h₄
  have h₂ : {x | p' x ∧ q' x} ∈ 𝓝 x := by
    have h₃ : {x | p' x} ∈ 𝓝 x := hT'
    have h₄ : {x | q' x} ∈ 𝓝 x := hS'
    have h₅ : {x | p' x ∧ q' x} ∈ 𝓝 x := by
      have h₆ : {x | p' x ∧ q' x} = {x | p' x} ∩ {x | q' x} := by
        ext x
        simp
      rw [h₆]
      exact nhds.inter_mem h₃ h₄
    exact h₅
  have h₃ : {x | p x ∧ q x ∨ p' x ∧ q' x} = {x | p x ∧ q x} ∪ {x | p' x ∧ q' x} := by
    ext x
    simp [or_iff_not_imp_left]
    <;> tauto
  rw [h₃]
  exact nhds.union_mem h₁ h₂

have h1 : {x | p x ∧ q x} ∈ 𝓝 x := by
    -- Since {x | p x} and {x | q x} are in the neighborhood filter, their intersection is also in the neighborhood filter.
    have h1 : {x | p x} ∩ {x | q x} ∈ 𝓝 x := by
      -- Use the fact that the intersection of two sets in the neighborhood filter is in the neighborhood filter.
      apply Filter.inter_mem
      · exact hT
      · exact hS
    -- The set {x | p x ∧ q x} is the same as the intersection of {x | p x} and {x | q x}.
    have h2 : {x | p x ∧ q x} = {x | p x} ∩ {x | q x} := by
      ext x
      simp [and_imp]
      <;> tauto
    -- Therefore, {x | p x ∧ q x} is in the neighborhood filter.
    rw [h2] at *
    exact h1
  have h2 : {x | p' x ∧ q' x} ∈ 𝓝 x := by
    -- Similarly, {x | p' x ∧ q' x} is in the neighborhood filter.
    have h1 : {x | p' x} ∩ {x | q' x} ∈ 𝓝 x := by
      apply Filter.inter_mem
      · exact hT'
      · exact hS'
    have h2 : {x | p' x ∧ q' x} = {x | p' x} ∩ {x | q' x} := by
      ext x
      simp [and_imp]
      <;> tauto
    rw [h2] at *
    exact h1
  -- The union of two sets in the neighborhood filter is in the neighborhood filter.
  have h3 : {x | p x ∧ q x ∨ p' x ∧ q' x} = {x | p x ∧ q x} ∪ {x | p' x ∧ q' x} := by
    ext x
    simp [or_imp]
    <;> tauto
  rw [h3]
  -- Therefore, {x | p x ∧ q x ∨ p' x ∧ q' x} is in the neighborhood filter.
  apply Filter.mem_union
  · exact h1
  · exact h2

have h₁ : {x : α | p x ∧ q x} ∈ 𝓝 x := by
    have h₂ : {x : α | p x ∧ q x} = {x : α | p x} ∩ {x : α | q x} := by
      ext x
      simp [and.intro]
      <;>
      aesop
    rw [h₂]
    -- The intersection of two sets in the neighborhood filter is in the neighborhood filter.
    exact Filter.inter_mem hT hS
  have h₂ : {x : α | p' x ∧ q' x} ∈ 𝓝 x := by
    have h₃ : {x : α | p' x ∧ q' x} = {x : α | p' x} ∩ {x : α | q' x} := by
      ext x
      simp [and.intro]
      <;>
      aesop
    rw [h₃]
    -- The intersection of two sets in the neighborhood filter is in the neighborhood filter.
    exact Filter.inter_mem hT' hS'
  -- The union of two sets in the neighborhood filter is in the neighborhood filter.
  have h₃ : {x : α | p x ∧ q x ∨ p' x ∧ q' x} = {x : α | p x ∧ q x} ∪ {x : α | p' x ∧ q' x} := by
    ext x
    simp [or]
    <;>
    aesop
  rw [h₃]
  -- The union of two sets in the neighborhood filter is in the neighborhood filter.
  exact Filter.union_mem h₁ h₂

    have h6 : {x : α | p x ∧ q x} ∪ {x : α |

have h₁ : {x | p x ∧ q x} ∈ 𝓝 x := by
    -- Use the fact that the intersection of two neighborhoods is a neighborhood
    have h₁ : {x | p x} ∈ 𝓝 x := hT
    have h₂ : {x | q x} ∈ 𝓝 x := hS
    -- The intersection of the two sets is in the neighborhood filter
    have h₃ : {x | p x} ∩ {x | q x} ∈ 𝓝 x :=
      Filter.inter_mem h₁ h₂
    -- Simplify the intersection to get the set of x where p x ∧ q x
    have h₄ : {x | p x} ∩ {x | q x} = {x | p x ∧ q x} := by
      ext x
      simp [and_comm]
      <;>
      aesop
    -- Conclude that {x | p x ∧ q x} is in the neighborhood filter
    rw [h₄] at h₃
    exact h₃
  
  have h₂ : {x | p' x ∧ q' x} ∈ 𝓝 x := by
    have h₁ : {x | p' x} ∈ 𝓝 x := hT'
    have h₂ : {x | q' x} ∈ 𝓝 x := hS'
    have h₃ : {x | p' x} ∩ {x | q' x} ∈ 𝓝 x :=
      Filter.inter_mem h₁ h₂
    have h₄ : {x | p' x} ∩ {x | q' x} = {x | p' x ∧ q' x} := by
      ext x
      simp [and_comm]
      <;>
      aesop
    rw [h₄] at h₃
    exact h₃
  
  have h₃ : {x | p x ∧ q x ∨ p' x ∧ q' x} ∈ 𝓝 x := by
    -- Prove that the union of two neighborhoods is a neighborhood
    have h₄ : {x | p x ∧ q x} ∪ {x | p' x ∧ q' x} ∈ 𝓝 x :=
      Filter.union_mem h₁ h₂
    -- Simplify the union to match the target set
    have h₅ : {x | p x ∧ q x} ∪ {x | p' x ∧ q' x} = {x | p x ∧ q x ∨ p' x ∧ q' x} := by
      ext x
      simp [or_comm]
      <;>
      aesop
    -- Conclude the proof by rewriting the set
    rw [h₅] at h₄
    exact h₄
  
  exact h₃
successful_attempts:
have h1 : ∃ s ∈ 𝓝 x, ∀ y ∈ s, p y := by
    refine' ⟨{x | p x}, hT, _⟩
    intro y hy
    exact hy
  have h2 : ∃ s ∈ 𝓝 x, ∀ y ∈ s, p' y := by
    refine' ⟨{x | p' x}, hT', _⟩
    intro y hy
    exact hy
  have h3 : ∃ s ∈ 𝓝 x, ∀ y ∈ s, q y := by
    refine' ⟨{x | q x}, hS, _⟩
    intro y hy
    exact hy
  have h4 : ∃ s ∈ 𝓝 x, ∀ y ∈ s, q' y := by
    refine' ⟨{x | q' x}, hS', _⟩
    intro y hy
    exact hy
  -- Use the fact that the intersection of finitely many neighborhoods is a neighborhood
  -- to find a common neighborhood where all conditions are satisfied.
  filter_upwards [hT, hT', hS, hS'] with y hy1 hy2 hy3 hy4
  -- For each y in the common neighborhood, at least one of the two conditions must hold.
  have h5 : p y ∧ q y ∨ p' y ∧ q' y := by
    by_cases h : p y
    · -- Case: p y holds
      by_cases h' : q y
      · -- Subcase: q y holds
        exact Or.inl ⟨h, h'⟩
      · -- Subcase: q y does not hold
        exfalso
        -- If q y does not hold, then y is not in {x | q x}, contradicting hy3.
        have h6 : q y := by
          have h7 : y ∈ {x | q x} := hy3
          exact h7
        contradiction
    · -- Case: p y does not hold
      by_cases h' : p' y
      · -- Subcase: p' y holds
        by_cases h'' : q' y
        · -- Subsubcase: q' y holds
          exact Or.inr ⟨h', h''⟩
        · -- Subsubcase: q' y does not hold
          exfalso
          -- If q' y does not hold, then y is not in {x | q' x}, contradicting hy4.
          have h6 : q' y := by
            have h7 : y ∈ {x | q' x} := hy4
            exact h7
          contradiction
      · -- Subcase: p' y does not hold
        exfalso
        -- If p' y does not hold, then y is not in {x | p' x}, contradicting hy2.
        have h6 : p' y := by
          have h7 : y ∈ {x | p' x} := hy2
          exact h7
        contradiction
  -- The result follows directly from the cases considered.
  exact h5
